questions.prompt: Complete: "___ he finish work at five?"
questions.explanation: A question with he uses Does + base verb: Does he finish work at five? "Do he" uses the wrong auxiliary, "Is" belongs to the verb be (not to finish), and "Doing" is not an auxiliary.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word order: "___ early on school days."
questions.explanation: Adverbs of frequency go before the main verb: She always gets up early. "gets up always" puts the adverb after the verb, and the other orders break the phrasal verb "get up" or front the adverb unnaturally.

options.text: She doesn't goes to the gym.

questions.explanation: The error is (a): after doesn't the main verb must be the base form, so it should be "She doesn't go to the gym." The -s already sits on does — double-marking it ("doesn't goes") is wrong. (b), (c) and (d) all keep the base verb correctly.

questions.prompt: Complete the wh- question: "Where ___ your parents work?"
questions.explanation: "Your parents" is plural (= they), so the auxiliary is do: Where do your parents work? "does" is singular (he/she/it), and "are/is" belong to the verb be, not to work.

options.text: My sister is study every evening.

questions.explanation: "My sister" = she, and study (consonant + y) becomes studies: My sister studies every evening. "study" forgets the -s ending, "studys" is the wrong spelling, and "is study" wrongly mixes two verbs.

questions.prompt: Complete: "My friends never ___ late to class."
questions.explanation: "My friends" is plural (= they), so use the base verb even with a frequency adverb: My friends never come late. The adverb never doesn't change the verb form, so "comes" (third-person -s) is wrong here; "to come" and "coming" are not present-simple forms.
